authorities.name: U.S. Geological Survey (Geographic Names Information System)

species.commonName: Wiper (white bass x striped bass hybrid)

sources.title: State of Nevada Board of Wildlife Commissioners — Fishing Seasons CR 25-16 (Jan 1, 2026 – Dec 31, 2027)
sources.quotedText: Lahontan Reservoir. Open year around. Daily: 15 game fish of which not more than 5 may be black bass, 5 may be walleye, and 2 may be white bass or white bass hybrids (wiper) 15 inches total length or longer. White bass or white bass hybrids (wiper) under 15 inches may be included in the 15 game fish limit. Possession: 30 game fish of which not more than 10 may be black bass, 10 may be walleye, and 4 may be white bass or white bass hybrids (wiper) 15 inches total length or longer. Hours: Any hour of the day or night.

sources.title: Lahontan Reservoir — Nevada Department of Wildlife
sources.quotedText: Lahontan Reservoir is located along the Carson River and is part of the Bureau of Reclamation's Newlands Irrigation Project for watering farmland in Lahontan Valley.

sources.title: Lahontan Reservoir Angler Information Guide (2026) — NDOW
Mercury in Fish — Nevada Department of Wildlife
sources.quotedText: Carson River from Dayton to the Lahontan Dam and all water in the Lahontan Valley: No consumption of any fish

sources.title: Apply & Buy — Nevada Fishing Licenses — NDOW
sources.quotedText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.

sources.title: Lahontan Reservoir Topo Map — TopoZone (Lyon County, NV; Silver Springs South USGS quad; named reservoir feature at 39.3502518, -119.1267812, elev. ~4,157 ft)

regulations.humanSummary: Lahontan Reservoir is open to fishing year-round.

parameters.counting_rule: Of the 15 daily game fish, no more than 5 may be black bass, no more than 5 may be walleye, and no more than 2 may be white bass or white bass hybrids (wiper) that are 15 inches total length or longer; white bass/wiper under 15 inches count toward the general 15-fish limit with no separate cap. In possession (30 fish total), no more than 10 may be black bass, 10 may be walleye, and 4 may be white bass/wiper 15 inches or longer.

regulations.humanSummary: 15 game fish/day (30 in possession), with sub-limits: at most 5 black bass and 5 walleye per day (10 and 10 in possession), plus at most 2 white bass/wiper 15in+ per day (4 in possession) — smaller white bass/wiper count toward the general limit uncapped.
regulations.verbatimText: Limits — Daily: 15 game fish of which not more than 5 may be black bass, 5 may be walleye, and 2 may be white bass or white bass hybrids (wiper) 15 inches total length or longer. White bass or white bass hybrids (wiper) under 15 inches may be included in the 15 game fish limit. Possession: 30 game fish of which not more than 10 may be black bass, 10 may be walleye, and 4 may be white bass or white bass hybrids (wiper) 15 inches total length or longer.

parameters.note: This is not a minimum-keep size — it is the threshold that separates the capped 2-per-day / 4-in-possession sub-limit for larger white bass/wiper from the uncapped smaller fish, which instead count toward the general 15 game fish daily limit.

speciesTargets.speciesCommonName: Wiper (white bass x striped bass hybrid)

regulations.humanSummary: A 15-inch total-length threshold separates the limited-quantity white bass/wiper sub-limit from smaller fish that fall under the general daily game-fish limit.
regulations.verbatimText: 2 may be white bass or white bass hybrids (wiper) 15 inches total length or longer. White bass or white bass hybrids (wiper) under 15 inches may be included in the 15 game fish limit.

regulations.humanSummary: Fishing is allowed any hour of the day or night.
regulations.verbatimText: Hours: Any hour of the day or night.

parameters.note: State agencies advise against eating any fish caught from Lahontan Reservoir due to methylmercury contamination stemming from historic Comstock-era mining discharges into the Carson River drainage; catch-and-release is recommended.

regulations.humanSummary: Nevada health and wildlife agencies advise against eating any fish caught from Lahontan Reservoir due to mercury contamination.
regulations.verbatimText: Carson River from Dayton to the Lahontan Dam and all water in the Lahontan Valley: No consumption of any fish

parameters.note: Standard Nevada resident/nonresident fishing license required for anglers 12 and older.

regulations.humanSummary: A Nevada fishing license is required for anglers age 12 and older; no license required under age 12.
regulations.verbatimText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.
